summary: AI Context-Aware Documentation retest (post-FIX). BACKEND: 100% green — pytest 13 passed + 1 skipped (the skipped TestContextLoader test is an artefact of an earlier note created before fix and is benign). All four target endpoints return 200 with REAL prior-encounter context for the admin-tenant signed follow-up note (note=9c3156b4-aa79-4046-8816-401ea4d95596, patient=dea68339-3951-45b5-8d47-19700c5ffcc8): /prior-sections (cached:true on 2nd hit, full SOAP summaries from 2026-04-27), /since-last-diff (NPRS callout 4→6 worsened), /draft-sections (subjective_draft + plan_draft + rationale, claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929), /chart-brief (cached:true on 2nd hit, brief references Hannah's prior NPRS / HEP / McKenzie content — no longer empty-context). Collection-rename FIX confirmed: services/ai/router.py:130 and services/ai/context.py:58 both query db.clinical_follow_up_notes. FRONTEND: BLOCKED by an UNRELATED pre-existing bug — /api/appointments?patient_id=... returns 500 with a Pydantic validation error (response.0.provider_id: 'Input should be a valid string, input: None'), which prevents PatientDetail.jsx from rendering its tab list. Without the tab list the Billing tab cannot be selected, so ChartBriefCard never mounts; FollowUpNoteEditor likewise renders only the loading skeleton because the same patient context never resolves. AI feature components themselves are intact (correct data-testids verified in source: chart-brief-card, chart-brief-regenerate-btn, chart-brief-cached-badge, encounter-assist-panel, assist-prior-sections, assist-since-last, assist-draft-btn, assist-pull-{section}-btn).

minor.issue: 500 InternalServerError — Pydantic response validation: 'response.0.provider_id: Input should be a valid string, input: None'. NOT in scope of this iteration but is the root cause of the frontend blockage that prevented UI verification of ChartBriefCard / EncounterAssistPanel. Fix: either coerce null provider_id to '' / Optional[str] in the response model, or skip rows with null provider_id.

Still skipped — the test patient happens to have 0 notes in load_patient_context after exclusion. Not a bug; switch the fixture to a patient with ≥2 signed follow-up notes so the test actually exercises the exclude path.

ui_bugs.issue: Page stays on loading skeleton; tab list (data-testid='tab-billing' etc.) never renders. Root cause is an unhandled 500 from /api/appointments (see backend.minor). Because the tabs never render, ChartBriefCard (mounted under the Billing tab at line 1487) cannot be exercised end-to-end via the UI. Suggest defensive try/catch around the appointments fetch in PatientDetail so a single failing dependency does not block the whole page render.

Editor reaches network-idle but EncounterAssistPanel sidebar (lg:sticky) does not materialise during the run. Same root cause likely (page loader gate on patient context). Backend GET /api/patients/{id}/clinical/notes/{nid} returns 200 in logs, so the note itself is fetched fine — the gating is in PatientDetail-style chrome. Once /api/appointments is fixed, retest.

integration_issues.issue: Cannot verify cached-badge, regenerate-btn click, or 2nd-visit cached:true badge in the UI because Billing tab cannot be selected.
Cannot verify Draft S+P or Pull-in subjective populates interval_history textarea because the Assist Panel does not render.

action_items: FIX (HIGH, blocks UI verification of AI feature): /api/appointments returns 500 because response model rejects rows with provider_id=None. Either make provider_id Optional[str] in the appointment response model or filter/coerce None at serialize time. After fix, retest the full UI flow per the original review request — backend AI endpoints are already green.
MINOR: switch fixture in TestContextLoader::test_exclude_note_produces_different_hash to a patient that has ≥2 signed follow-up notes so the skip becomes a real assertion.

Once /api/appointments is fixed, the rest of the UI test (ChartBriefCard render + cached badge on 2nd visit + EncounterAssistPanel prior-sections/since-last-callouts + Draft S+P + Pull-in subjective) should pass given the backend already returns the right data — main agent CAN self-test by selecting Billing tab on /patients/dea68339-3951-45b5-8d47-19700c5ffcc8 and opening note 9c3156b4-aa79-4046-8816-401ea4d95596.
critical_code_review_comments: Collection-name fix is in place and correct: services/ai/router.py:130 db.clinical_follow_up_notes.find_one(...) and services/ai/context.py:58 db.clinical_follow_up_notes.find(notes_q) both query the right collection — confirmed by happy-path 200 responses with real prior SOAP content.
ai_usage audit row (TestUsageAudit) continues to scrub prompt/response bodies — model + latency_ms + status only. Good.
Suggest adding a Mongo collection-name constant (e.g. FOLLOW_UP_NOTES_COLL) imported by both ai/* and clinical/* modules to prevent regressions of the iteration_78 root cause.
ChartBriefCard and EncounterAssistPanel component code/testids look clean — no UI bug intrinsic to those components; the blockage is upstream in PatientDetail's hard dependency on /api/appointments.

PatientDetail.jsx should be hardened: a failed fetch on a single tile (appointments) should not gate the entire tab chrome. Currently every navigation to /patients/{id} is broken if appointments 500s — high blast radius.

success_rate.backend: 100% (13/13 pytest pass, 1 benign skip; 4/4 AI endpoints return 200 with real data via curl)
success_rate.frontend: 0% verified (blocked by unrelated /api/appointments 500 bug; AI components & testids exist correctly in source but cannot be reached via UI)

test_credentials: admin@ccms.app / Admin@ComplianceClinic1 (from /app/memory/test_credentials.md). Test note: 9c3156b4-aa79-4046-8816-401ea4d95596 (admin tenant a5b12dcb-88da-4121-b0b0-1fafac646b7e), patient dea68339-3951-45b5-8d47-19700c5ffcc8.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend AI endpoints are fully fixed and return 200 with real Hannah/NPRS context — verified via 4 curl calls with admin cookie against note 9c3156b4-aa79-4046-8816-401ea4d95596 / patient dea68339-3951-45b5-8d47-19700c5ffcc8. The ONLY blocker for completing the UI portion of this review is /api/appointments returning 500 (Pydantic provider_id None rejection), which puts PatientDetail.jsx into a permanent skeleton state. Once that 500 is fixed, run: (1) login admin → /patients/dea68339-3951-45b5-8d47-19700c5ffcc8 → click [data-testid='tab-billing'] → scroll → assert [data-testid='chart-brief-card'] body length >500 chars and contains domain words; reload → assert [data-testid='chart-brief-cached-badge'] visible. (2) Navigate to /patients/dea68339-.../clinical/follow-up/9c3156b4-... → assert [data-testid='encounter-assist-panel'] → assert assist-prior-sections + assist-since-last populated → click [data-testid='assist-draft-btn'] (wait ~25s) → assert [data-testid='assist-draft'] now contains drafts → click [data-testid='assist-pull-subjective-btn'] → assert interval_history textarea grows. (3) regression smoke routes /communications/sms /settings/email /settings/google-auth — those need their own re-verification once UI is unblocked.
rca of the issue: Iteration_78 collection-name root cause is fully resolved (verified by 4 successful curl calls with real prior-SOAP content). New blocker for UI verification is unrelated: /api/appointments response model rejects rows where provider_id is None (Pydantic v2 string_type error), causing the GET to 500 and PatientDetail.jsx to remain on its skeleton state, which in turn prevents the Billing tab (and therefore ChartBriefCard) from mounting. Mitigation: change provider_id to Optional[str] in the appointment response schema, or substitute None with empty string at the serializer. Reproduction: login as admin → curl /api/appointments?patient_id=dea68339-3951-45b5-8d47-19700c5ffcc8 → 500 with response.0.provider_id validation error (visible in /var/log/supervisor/backend.*.log).
